what_it_does: Records a recovered decision about which embedding model to use for past-session and transcript search, documenting the chosen model, rejected alternatives, GPU/voice coexistence rationale, serving complications, and loadout context so the decision is not lost again.
observations: Recovered verbatim from session bda8ce28 line references after an agent failed to recall the decision.
Chosen transcript embedder is pplx-embed-context-v1-4b with a jina reranker on CPU.
Qwen3-Embedding-8B is held as the quality ceiling and bge-m3 is the already-running bootstrap/fallback.
The 4B model was chosen over the 8B specifically to leave GPU headroom for voice models on a 16 GB GPU.
pplx-4b is not in vLLM's supported architectures and its custom code targets an older transformers, so serving required in-session workarounds.
In-session services built: embed-pplx on port 8007, jina reranker, claude-sessions-reindex and agent-sessions-exporter timers bound to Company lifecycle.
The broader vision is a purpose-loadout rather than a single embedding winner.
The file marks embedding-serving, GPU loadout, and voice as the lead's locked lane and notes it was relayed to the lead.
standouts: The decision was recovered because an agent forgot it and caused user frustration.
A quality-vs-GPU tradeoff: the #1 MTEB 8B model is reserved so voice models can coexist on the GPU.
pplx-4b already hit real serving complications in the same session.
The file explicitly exists so the decision is never lost again.
conventions: Uses COMMIT-GRAMMAR/session-store-grammar style metadata header (trust, author, date, verified, recovers).
Cites verbatim transcript line numbers as evidentiary anchors.
Uses wikilink references to related records and concepts.
Written as a neutral, file-local decision record in the recall/ directory.
Avoids ranking alternatives, instead describing roles (chosen, quality ceiling, bootstrap).
concerns: Heavy dependence on a specific session transcript and line numbers that may become stale.
The chosen model is described as interim/throwaway, so this record may need updating soon.
Serving pplx-4b required unsupported-architecture and older-transformers workarounds.
The 8B quality ceiling cannot currently run alongside voice on the same 16 GB GPU.
notes: A recovered session decision that locks the transcript embedder to pplx-4b plus a CPU reranker because the 8B model leaves no GPU headroom for voice.
questions: What is the exact GPU and how are voice models quantized or offloaded?
Is the embed-pplx service currently running, and what is its uptime/health?
How is the jina reranker invoked and what latency does it add?
What mechanism binds the timers to the Company lifecycle?
What do the linked records [[mode-loadout-registry]], [[native-model-layer]], and [[voice-stack]] contain?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to preserve a recovered decision so it is never lost again, and the file does exactly that.
apparent_themes: GPU budget and model coexistence constraints
intention_signals: It is trying to prevent a previously forgotten decision from being lost again by anchoring it in a durable, searchable ledger with provenance and reasoning, while signaling that execution belongs to the lead's lane.
novelty: It is a recovered-decision artifact produced by session-recall capability, explicitly created because an agent failed to recall the decision earlier, and it uses verbatim transcript line citations as evidentiary anchors.
contribution: It contributes a durable, provenance-rich record of an embedding-model choice and its GPU/voice coexistence rationale, which a fused system would keep as a decision audit trail and as context for future loadout and serving discussions.
summary_for_embedding: Recovered decision record from session bda8ce28 selecting pplx-embed-context-v1-4b plus a CPU jina reranker for past-session transcript search on a 16 GB GPU, with Qwen3-Embedding-8B held as a quality ceiling and bge-m3 as the running bootstrap.
intention_for_embedding: Preserve the embedding-model decision and its GPU/voice coexistence rationale so it is never lost again and can be referenced by future loadout and recall queries.
